comment.body: My assessment is that Putin's strategy serves mainly for him to stay in power (the miscalculation was too big); of course Putin is betting on a divided and weakened West. In any case, this playing is hardly good for Russia; the longer it continues the longer Russia risks becoming a Chinese vassal.

comment.body: The thing is I don't see what he can achieve. He most likely miscalculated in 2022 that it would be locally contained like Abkhazia/South Ossetia in 2008, or Crimea/Donbass in 2014... Realistically, Putin cannot afford not remaining in power before his last breath; he doesn't have a golden parachute...

comment.body: Geopolitically speaking, China and Russia don't trust each other (for example, they compete for dominance in Central Asia, and parts of Siberia once belonged to China etc.), and Russia is too proud to be a Chinese vassal. It's an alliance of convenience for the time being. It's just that Putin doesn't have a choice...
